summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Mime::Type.parse exhibits 36445x amplification when processing a crafted Accept header with 20000 repeated text/* wildcard entries (140KB), consuming 0.66s of CPU on a single request versus 0.000018s for a normal Accept header.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a single crafted HTTP Accept header with many wildcard MIME types causes disproportionately high CPU consumption in Mime::Type.parse, confirming an unauthenticated algorithmic-efficiency denial-of-service vector.
actual_observed_result: Running the Docker container with the target Rails 7.2.3 source mounted at /target-repo, the verify.rb script loaded Mime::Type.parse and measured: normal Accept header (53 bytes) = 0.000018s, crafted Accept header (140000 bytes, 20000 text/* entries) = 0.658s. Amplification ratio = 36445.2x. The proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF:accept_header_wildcard_amplification_dos_confirmed was emitted, confirming successful reproduction.
verification_method: The verify.rb script loads the actual Rails Mime::Type.parse code from the target repository, benchmarks a normal Accept header (10 iterations averaged) against a crafted header with 20000 text/* wildcard entries, and confirms the vulnerability if the crafted header processing exceeds 0.5 seconds. The proof string distinguishes a real timing amplification from a startup failure or parsing error.
evidence_produced_by_run: Normal Accept header (53 bytes): 0.000018s per parse
Crafted Accept header (139999 bytes, 20000 text/* entries): 0.658s per parse

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
